rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract definitions.
generic_rules: For component type contracts, place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Use eq declarations for boolean range checks: eq name : bool = expression.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume/guarantee syntax within annex agree blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
